01_initial_request.input_data: Glass blanks from Ohara, Japan; Science case requirements (Skidmore et al. 2015)
01_initial_request.user_intent: Develop and contribute to TMT telescope and instruments to enable advanced astronomical observations and maximize scientific productivity
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Final surface figure error < 2 nm for primary mirror segments

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Glass blanks from Ohara, Japan are available as input material for the TMT primary mirror. [Gap] The roundels need to be shaped to match the theoretical surface before segmentation. [Decision] To achieve the initial optical surface specification, chose to use Stress mirror polishing to perform polishing on roundels, expecting to obtain optical surface figure errors < 1.73 μm peak-to-valley (P-V).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Roundels are polished to a shape described by its unique Zernike terms, to a level that the optical surface figure errors relative to the theoretical surface is < 1.73 μm peak-to-valley (P-V).

references.relevance: Provides science case requirements driving the telescope specifications.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Polished roundels are ready for segmentation. [Gap] The circular roundels must be converted into hexagonal segments to form the primary mirror array. [Decision] To achieve the segment geometry, chose to use Hex-cutting process to perform cutting on polished roundels, expecting to obtain hexagonal segments ~1.44 m across corners.

parameters.geometry: not regular hexagons and they are also not identical

02_agent_trajectory.observation: The polished roundels are hex-cut into hexagons ~1.44 m as measured across corners. However, the segments are not regular hexagons and they are also not identical.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hex-cut segments require further surface refinement. [Gap] Specific surface roughness is needed for optical performance. [Decision] To achieve the required surface roughness, chose to use Ion Beam Figuring (IBF) to perform figuring on hex-cut segments, expecting to obtain surface roughness of 20 Å RMS.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: At this stage, the required surface roughness of both front and back surfaces need to be 20 Å RMS that includes all features with a spatial period between 20 and 800 μm.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Segments are polished and figured. [Gap] Segments need to be mounted to maintain position with minimal distortion. [Decision] To achieve stable segment positioning, chose to use Segment Support Assembly (SSA) integration to perform mounting on segments, expecting to obtain minimal gravity distortion.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: First, SSA is successfully integrated at the Indian vendor facility and meets the stringent requirements.

references.relevance: References the fabrication of SSA and M1CS carried out by ITCC.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Segments are mounted on SSAs. [Gap] Overall mirror shape must be maintained despite environmental deformations. [Decision] To achieve shape maintenance, chose to use Primary Mirror Control System (M1CS) to perform active control on segments, expecting to obtain nanometer-level feedback correction.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The primary mirror control system (M1CS) maintains the overall shape of the segmented primary mirror despite structural deformations caused by temperature, gravity and disturbances from wind and other vibrations.

references.relevance: References the fabrication of SSA and M1CS carried out by ITCC.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] IBF and SSA corrections are applied. [Gap] Final optical quality must meet the TMT quantifiable goal. [Decision] To achieve the final specification, chose to use Surface figure error measurement to perform verification on primary mirror segments, expecting to obtain final surface figure error < 2 nm.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The final surface figure error, which is < 2 nm after IBF and SSA corrections need to be achieved. ITCC has already successfully completed polishing a segment at the training site in USA.

references.relevance: Provides science case requirements driving the telescope specifications.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Surface figure error measurement and training site polishing completion

final_surface_figure_error.interpretation: Target requirement for primary mirror segments after IBF and SSA corrections

initial_polishing_error.interpretation: Optical surface figure errors relative to theoretical surface for roundels

surface_roughness.interpretation: Required surface roughness of both front and back surfaces

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The process is on track with successful completion of segment polishing at the training site and SSA integration meeting stringent requirements, aiming for the < 2 nm final surface figure error goal.
